raw:
I'll examine the changed files and the referenced sibling modules to judge the work.
NIT 1 and NIT 2 look good. Now let me check the guard_test.sh and index.ts.
NIT 3 satisfied. Now let me check index.ts for NIT 4.
All four nits verified against the actual code:

**NIT 1** — `engagement.ts:1-7` has a concise top-of-file block comment above the imports, matching the sibling-module voice (pure/node-builtin-only, out-of-tree at `<agentDir>/foreman/engagement.json`, only OFF overrides, default ON, no pi imports → headlessly testable). ✓

**NIT 2** — `engagement.ts:106-109` adds a JSDoc above `setRepoEngagement` documenting that it persists and returns the freshly-read `RepoEngagement`. Return type kept as `RepoEngagement`, behavior unchanged. ✓

**NIT 3** — `guard_test.sh:117` uses `fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "foreman-engagement-"))`; `delete process.env.FOREMAN_ENGAGEMENT_STORE` at the end (line ~243) plus `fs.rmSync(..., { recursive: true, force: true })` cleanup (and an `on("exit")` safety net). No tmp under HOME; assertions unchanged. ✓

**NIT 4** — `index.ts` `tool_call` handler now: crew bypass first, computes `cwdGitRoot`/`root` once, reads `repoEngagementActive(root)` a single time into `engagementActive`, reuses it for both `setForemanDirectStatus(ctx, engagementActive)` and the gating decision. `setForemanDirectStatus` refactored to `(ctx, active: boolean)`, and all three other callers (session_start, `/foreman-direct`, engage param) pass the already-computed boolean. NON_GIT hint still keyed off `cwdGitRoot`; status text identical. Single store read per call. ✓

Constraints respected: `engagement.ts` imports only node builtins (no pi import), `guard.ts` untouched, append-only system-prompt mechanism untouched, persistence format and status text unchanged. Full suite green (verify exit 0). No cheats (no hardcoding, tests not weakened).

VERDICT: PASS
